headlines.title: Tehran suspends talks with U.S. over Israeli attacks in Lebanon, Iranian media reports
headlines.description: Iran on Monday suspended talks with the United States to protest Israel’s expanding offensive in Lebanon, according to government-aligned media.

headlines.title: Sen. Chuck Schumer lays out plan to fight Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund in Congress
headlines.description: The Senate minority leader said Democrats would use Senate procedure to try to kill President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, forcing Republicans to vote to preserve the compensation money.

headlines.title: Florida sues OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman, accusing them of putting profit over safety
headlines.description: Florida’s attorney general filed a lawsuit against OpenAI and the company’s CEO, saying the company misrepresents safety to turn a profit.

headlines.title: How shoppers who pay in cash are subsidizing Americans’ credit card reward points
headlines.description: A Harvard study estimates that people who pay with cash and debit cards are subsidizing $30 billion a year in points and perks for credit card users.

headlines.title: Hospitals in Brazil Test Two Patients With Ebola-Like Symptoms
headlines.description: The director general of the World Health Organization has arrived in the Democratic Republic of Congo as doctors on the ground race to play catch up against the third largest Ebola outbreak in history. In Rio De Janeiro and São Paulo, Brazil, hospitals are testing two patients with Ebola-like symptoms. NBC’s Molly Hunter reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Primary challengers threaten to unseat House members across the country
headlines.description: Four House members have already lost re-election bids so far this year, just a few months into primary season.

headlines.title: A personalized vaccine for melanoma cut the risk of cancer returning after five years
headlines.description: An experimental vaccine from Moderna shows promise in keeping deadly skin cancer from returning for years, according to new clinical trial results.

headlines.title: Red stripes declared U.K.’s oldest art after being dismissed as a natural phenomenon
headlines.description: Dismissed as a natural phenomenon for more than a century, red stripes on a rock in Wales have been found to be the oldest known prehistoric art in Britain.

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Around 1 in 5 young people use AI chatbots for mental health advice, survey finds
headlines.description: Nearly 1 in 5 adolescents and young adults are turning to AI chatbots for advice when they’re sad, angry, nervous or stressed, according to a new study.

headlines.title: Oil prices jump after U.S.-Iran talks reportedly collapse, erasing recent declines
headlines.description: The price of oil rose sharply after Iran reportedly cut off talks with the United States to the war. Crude oil jumped to levels not seen since mid-May.

headlines.title: M.I.A. sues Kid Cudi, saying she was dropped from tour for ‘publicity’ amid low ticket sales
headlines.description: British rapper M.I.A. filed a multi-million-dollar lawsuit last week against Kid Cudi, accusing him of dropping her from his tour “to generate publicity” for shows that’ve “struggled with ticket sales.”
